ety: Prk. तउ, gen. sing. base त or तु; see tū
def: The formative, or base (in Braj. &c.) of the dat., acc., and abl. cases of the pron. tū (e.g. to-hi = tujh-ko); — thy, thine ( = terā).
ex: اے خدا تو ہی میرا مددگار ہے!
ai xudā tū-hī merā madadgār hai!
O God, only Thou art my helper!
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुहुं (tuhũ), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑀁 (tumaṃ), 𑀢𑀼𑀁 (tuṃ), from Sanskrit त्वम् (tvam).

r: t̤o, e, t̤ā, t̤āʼě-mǒhmala, t̤āʼě-g̠air-manqūt̤a, te, t̤abar-zad, tabar-zad, t, ṭa, ḍa, ṛa
def: (called t̤āʼě-mǒhmala or t̤āʼě-g̠air-manqūt̤a), the twenty-second letter of the Urdū alphabet (the sixteenth letter of the Arabic, from which it is taken). The letter is purely Arabic or Semitic; but it is sometimes used in certain Persian words for an original ت te (e.g. t̤abar-zad for tabar-zad, 'sugar-candy'). In Arabic it is a strongly articulated palatal t; but in Hindūstānī and Persian its sound is hardly distinguishable from that of ت te. As a numeral it stands for 9; and in almanacs it represents the sign Capricorn. In Urdū manuscripts it is commonly used instead of the four dots or points over the letters ٿ ṭa, ڐ ḍa, and ڙ ṛa (e.g. ٹ &c.).

conj. (correl. to agar, jo, jab) & adv. Therefore, then, in that case; at any rate, at least; at that time; moreover; that; also; for; — yes, well! — (as a particle of asseveration or emphasis, to = ) indeed, actually, in point of fact; forsooth; just (cf. the S. adv. tu)
subs: (S. tad+api), conj. Even then, even so, yet, still, nevertheless, notwithstanding
adv. Forsooth; just (more emphatic than simple to)
tau-hī, adv. (Braj) = tab-hī, and tau-bhī, qq.v.
ex: اے خدا تو ہی میرا مددگار ہے!
ai xudā tū-hī merā madadgār hai!
O God, only Thou art my helper!

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تُهْمَت (tuhmat), borrowed from Arabic تُهْمَة (tuhma). First attested in c. 1682 as Middle Hindi تہمت (thmt /⁠tuhmat⁠/).

def: s.m. Weighing; weight or quantity measured by the balance; standard weight or measure of weight

s.m. A weight of 12 (or 16) māshas (esp. of gold or silver), the weight of the Sicca rupee, or about 179⅔ grains; (the tola is reckoned in books at 16 māshas of 6½ grains each, and weighs therefore 184 grains Troy; in practice, however, it is calculated at 12 māshas, jeweller's weight, and weighs nearly 210 grains); — a weigher of coins, &c.; weighman
s.m. = تولا tolā, q.v.

s.m. A quarter or district of a town; a part of a town inhabited by men of one class, sect, or trade; — a set (of anything), a complete assortment; — a piece of rock, a stone, a large pebble; a knock, stroke.
s.m. A weight (see tolā); a weighman.
s.f. A band, body, &c. ( = ṭol); a quarter of a town, &c. ( = ṭolā); a heavy stone.

ety: tol˚ = Prk. तोल(इ) or तोले(इ) = S. तोल(ति) or तोलय(ति), rt. तुल्
def: v.t. To weigh; to balance; to ponder, deliberate; to judge, estimate; to confront one another (as two armies).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀮𑀤𑀺 (taladi), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *talati. Compare Punjabi ਤਲਣਾ (talṇā), Gujarati તળવું (taḷvũ), Marathi तळणे (taḷṇe), Odia ତଳିବା (taḷibā).

ety: for A. طعمة, n. of un. fr. t̤ǒʻm, inf. n. of طعم 'to eat'
def: s.m. Food; dinner, a meal, repast; bait (thrown to fish, &c.); grain, &c. (thrown to birds).

s.m. A myriad, ten-thousand; a sum of money equal to ten thousand Arabic silver drachmas (which are about one-third less than those of the Greeks); a sum of fifteen dollars and a half; districts into which a kingdom is divided (each being supposed to furnish 10,000 fighting men).
s.m. An iron club or crow; a lance; a javelin; name of a class of Rājpūts ( = tuwar, taṅwar); a kind of metre consisting of four lines of nine syllables each.
s.f. = tūmṛī and toṅbṛī, qq.v.
s.f. = tūmṛī and toṅbṛī, qq.v.
s.m. Name of a class of Rājpūts ( = tomar).
s.m. The gourd Lagenaria vulgaris in a dry state; the same with the inner part scooped out (used by mendicants to carry water in, &c., and also as floats in swimming, &c.).

s.f. = tomṛā, q.v.; (tūmṛī) a musical instrument made of the gourd, a kind of pipe (used by snake-charmers and jugglers); the snout of an alligator, or crocodile, &c. (so called from its resemblance to the shape of the gourd); (tomṛī or tūmṛī) a lantern made of a hollowed gourd, or of a hollowed water-melon, or of earth, &c.; a kind of firework (a small earthen pot filled with powder, &c.; syn. phul-jharī).
adv. In that manner, so, thus; then, in that case ( = to; tab, q.v.); also = tyoṅ, q.v.
subs: adv. In that very manner, exactly so; — exactly then, at that very instant, there and then; then even.

def: v.t. = ṭohnā, q.v.
s.m. Charm, spell, enchantment (cf. ṭoṭkā), necromancy, magic, witchcraft; fascination; a marriage song
subs: (-ke), To nullify or ward off enchantments or spells
s.m. An enchanter, magician, necromancer, wizard, conjurer, juggler ( = ṭonhā)
s.m. = ṭonā-ṭānī, s.f. Charms and spells; hocus-pocus, juggling.

def: s.m. = tumbā; tomṛā, qq.v.
s.f. = tumbī; tomṛī, tūmṛī, qq.v.
s.m. = tumbā; tomṛā, qq.v.
s.f. = tumbī; tomṛī, tūmṛī, qq.v.
s.f. A large belly, a pot-belly; corpulence.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *तोंद (toṃda), तुंद n (tuṃda), from Sanskrit तुन्द n (tunda).

def: adj. & s.m. Pot-bellied, gorbellied, corpulent; — a gorbellied person.
adj. & s.m. Pot-bellied, gorbellied, corpulent; — a gorbellied person.
adj. & s.m. = toṅdailā.
adj. & s.m. = toṅdailā.
s.f. The navel ( = tonḍī).
s.f. A finger-tip.
s.f. The navel.
s.f. The navel ( = toṅdī).
adj. & s.m. = toṅdailā.
s.f. Point; end, &c. = ṭoṅk, q.v.
s.m. One who casts spells, a necromancer, sorcerer, wizard, magician, conjurer, juggler; one who professes to cure diseases by means of charms or superstitious remedies.
s.f. Enchantress, sorceress, witch, female conjurer, &c. (see ṭonhā).
s.f. lit. 'A beak or bill'; point (of a weapon, tool, pen, &c.); end, extremity, limit, extreme (of a building, or a town or village, or a strip of land, &c.).
s.f. = ṭok, and ṭhoṅg, qq.v.
s.f. The beak or bill of a bird ( = ćoṅć)
subs: v.n. lit. 'To become like a bill'; to become rigid, be benumbed or cramped.

def: s.m. = ṭoṭā, q.v.; — adj. Handless.
s.f. A spout
subs: ṭoṅṭī-wālā, adj. Having a spout; — ṭoṅṭī-wālā loṭā, s.m. An ewer.

def: s.m. Charm, &c. ( = ṭonā); — a species of hawk.

ety: T. tob, top, orig. 'a round mass' = S. stūpa
def: s.f. A gun, cannon, field-piece; mortar, howitzer; — (fig.) a fat or corpulent person
subs: v.t. To blow away from a gun ( = top-se uṛānā)
s.m. Artillery; the artillery (in an army); a battery, a park of artillery; the place where cannon and artillery stores are kept, an arsenal
s.m. Fire-arms, muskets, rifles (as opposed to cannon)
v.t. To blow away from a gun; to condemn to be shot
top mārnā, v.n. = top ćalānā, q.v.

def: s.m. Heap, pile; cluster; a Buddhist monument, a tumulus erected over a sacred spot (as the top of Manikiāla in the Panjāb); a cluster of trees, a grove, plantation, garden.
s.m. A Buddhist monument (a kind of tumulus erected over sacred relics of the great Buddha, or on spots consecrated as the scenes of his acts).

s.m. A covering for the head; a cornered cap (lined with cotton) which covers the ears and the back of the head; a kind of hood; a hat, helmet; cover, head, or cap (of a thing); a thimble; a drop; bait; a long stitch (such as is used in basting or tacking, cf. ṭappā).

s.m. A covering for the head; a cornered cap (lined with cotton) which covers the ears and the back of the head; a kind of hood; a hat, helmet; cover, head, or cap (of a thing); a thimble; a drop; bait; a long stitch (such as is used in basting or tacking, cf. ṭappā).
v.t. = tupānā, q.v.
s.m. A conductor of artillery; a commissary of ordnance; a bombardier; a cannonier.
s.f. A hat, cap; cap (of a gun); cover or cap (as of a telescope, &c.); head or top (of a thing)
subs: s.m. One who wears a hat; a European, (also, in former times, a Persian or a Mogul soldier); an animal with a comb, or a crest, &c.

def: v.t. To cover with a top or mound, to bury; to cover; to conceal.
v.t. To cover with earth, to bury; to set (a tree or cutting), to plant; to sow with the hand; to hang, suspend; to baste, to tack (in which sense it is connected with thāp, q.v.).
s.m. A fly; — a kind of pigeon.
s.f. A net thrown over a woman's pālkī.

ety: Prk. तोड़ो, S. त्रोटः; see toṛnā
def: s.m. Breaking; break, interruption; fracture, rupture; breach, rent, fissure; breaking or injuring power (of a gun, &c.); force or velocity (of a current, &c.); method of warding off or meeting (a blow, &c.), guard, defence; efficacy, effect, virtue (of a medicine, &c.); effective or telling rejoinder, answer, refutation; turning point, height, climax (of heat, cold, disease, &c.); irrigation of fields by flooding (i.e. by breaking down a dam, or the ridges of water-courses); whey; — a net thrown over a child's cradle, or a woman's pālkī
subs: toṛ-tāṛ, s.m. Breaking and smashing; breaking to pieces; fracture, rupture; breach; injury, damage, destruction, ravage, havoc; effectiveness; plain-speaking; winning over, allurement, enticement

(and joṛ-toṛ), s.m. Breaking and joining; breaking (with one) and taking up (with another); taking to pieces and reconstructing (a machine); arrangement (of words in composition), construction (of a sentence), combination, cutting out and putting together (a garment); contriving, managing, effecting through shifts and expedients; deliberation, planning; plans, contrivances, schemes, devices, expedients, shifts
(-meṅ), To make a breach (in, as a ball)

ety: Prk. तुह+gen. aff. rā; see to and tu
def: adj. (dialec.), Thy, thine( = terā).
s.m. = توره tora, q.v.

ety: prob. an Aryan word in a Semitic form
def: s.m. A tray or dish of various meats; a number of trays containing various dishes of food (presented by the rich on marriage and other occasions); — a nobleman; a minister; — pride, haughtiness, conceit, airs (cf. toṛā)
subs: s.f. The arrangement or the sending out of trays of food as presents
(-ko), To become proud, couceited, or vain, &c.

def: s.m. Break, interruption, exhaustion, deficiency, scarcity, want; a piece of rope or cord; a plough-share; the match of a gun, a linstock; a bank, bar, an island (syn. ćar); a purse, a bag of one thousand rupees (in this sense prob. derived from S. tola+ka); a gold or silver chain for the neck, a collarette (usually consisting of four or five separate chains); a similar ornament for the ankles (usually corsisting of three chains); — name of a rāg or musical mode
subs: s.f. Wrenching and twisting: pinching and squeezing
s.m., toṛe-dār bandūk, s.f. A matchlock; a gun of great range or damaging power.

def: s.m. = toṛā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of anklet with bells (worn by women and children; cf. toṛā).
s.m. = ṭoṛā, q.v.
s.m. A ledge (without pillars) extending out from a wall (to keep off the weather); eaves; battens of a roof.
s.m. Custom, law, rule, authority, order, institute, regulation, enactment (e.g. sharʻ tore-kī zan hai).

ety: Prk. तुह+gen. aff. rā; see to and tu
def: adj. (dialec.), Thy, thine( = terā).

ety: toṛa+l = Prk. डो = S. रः or लः
def: s.m. A large thick ring of gold or silver, &c. worn on the wrist or ankle.
s.m. An arch, gateway; the ornamented arch of a door or gateway; the decorations of a gateway; strings of flowers suspended across gateways on festive occasions (syn. bandanwār).
v.t. = tuṛānā, tup wānā, q.v.
s.m. = turhī, q.v.
s.f. The name of a rāginī or musical mode.
(prov.), postpn. To, up to, till ( = tak).
s.f. = turaʼī, q.v.
s.f. & postpn. = toṛī, q.v.
s.f. The mustard plant; mustard-seed: rape-seed; — name of a rāginī or musical mode.
s.f. The mustard plant; mustard-seed: rape-seed; — name of a rāginī or musical mode.

ety: caus. of ṭūṭnā; — toṛ˚ = Prk. तोड(इ) or तोडे(इ) = S. त्रोटय(ति), caus. of rt. त्रुट्y
def: v.t. To break, tear, rend, burst, split, crack; to interrupt, cut off, stop, discontinue, abolish, to exhaust; to sever, sunder (a tie, or friendship, &c.); to destroy, demolish; to ruin (a person); to violate (a vow), infringe (a canon); to break into (a house), force or break open (a lock); to break up (ground), to plough; to pluck or gather (fruit, &c.); to stretch, strain, exercise (the body, as by gymnastics); to take, capture, conquer (a stronghold); to demolish (an argument), to refute, confute; to change (money); to reduce (in Arith.)
subs: v.t. 'To break and join'; to take to pieces and reconstruct; to make and mar
(intens. of toṛnā), v.t. To break to pieces, to break and destroy, to pull down, to demolish, &c.
v.t. To gather or pluck (fruit, &c.); to draw off or away, to sunder, to bring over, gain or win over
v.t. To wrench and twist; to pinch and squeeze
To be at the point of death
To eat the bread of idleness.

def: s.m. A dried pod of toṛī (q.v.) kept for seed.
s.f. tuṛwāʼī, q.v.
v.t. = tuṛwānā, q.v.
s.f. = toshak, q.v.
s.m. corr. of tosh-dān, q.v.
s.m. Satisfaction, gratification, contentment, pleasure, joy, happiness; contentment personified as a son of Bhagavat and one of the twelve Tushitas.

s.m. Provision for a journey, provision supplies, viaticum; provision for a day accompanying the funeral of a deceased person (to support him during his journey to the other world, — usually, however, given as alms to the grave-digger, or to some poor person); food placed as an offering at the tomb of a saint
subs: (prop. toshak-ḵẖāna), s.m. A place where furniture is kept, a wardrobe; store-house, store-room
s.m. Provision for the next world, laying up treasure in heaven; good deeds; a child gone to heaven; bearing with resignation the loss of a child.

def: s.m. Bedding; a quilt, a mattress; (in Pers.) furniture, effects, &c.
subs: s.m. A place where furniture is kept, &c. (com. tosha-ḵẖāna, q.v.).

def: adj. & s.m. Gratifying, causing pleasure or happiness; — one who, or that which, causes pleasure, &c.
adj. & s.m. Satisfying, gratifying, making contented, appeasing, pleasing; — the act of satisfying; satisfaction; gratification (of), &c.
s.m. A pouch; a cartridge or cartouche box.
adj. & s.m. Satisfied or contented (with), pleased (with); satisfying or making contented, gladdening; — a contented person, &c.

ety: tosh˚ = S. तोषय(ति), rt. तुष्+nā = ana = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To satisfy, gratify, please, &c.

ety: P. tota; S. थुत्+अकः; cf. totlā and toto
def: s.m. A parrot; — pet, darling (a term of endearment applied to children)
subs: v.n. To talk like a parrot, to prattle or talk sweetly
(-ke, or hathoṅ-ke), To lose one's power of prating, to be struck dumb, to be confounded
v.n. To turn away one's faithless eyes (to another), to withdraw the affections (from), to become indifferent (to).
wety: Possibly from Classical Persian توته (tōta, “parrot”), طوطی (tūtī). Compare Punjabi ਤੋਤਾ (totā) / طوطا (t̤ot̤ā), Sindhi طوطو (t̤ot̤o) / तोतो (toto).

def: s.m. The hammer or cock of a matchlock.
wety: Possibly from Classical Persian توته (tōta, “parrot”), طوطی (tūtī). Compare Punjabi ਤੋਤਾ (totā) / طوطا (t̤ot̤ā), Sindhi طوطو (t̤ot̤o) / तोतो (toto).

def: s.m. = totā, q.v.
s.m. Loss, damage, injury, detriment; deficiency, scarcity, lack; — a hollow tube of bamboo; a cartridge; a cracker or squib (straight); a candle-end
subs: v.n. To suffer loss, to lose, to sustain injury or damage
(-kā), To make good a loss or damage, &c.; to make restitution, pay compensation, to indemnify, to refund
s.f. Making good a loss, &c., paying compensation; compensation, indemnification, restitution
v.n. A loss to befall; to suffer or incur loss; to fail ( = ṭoṭā uṭhānā)

def: s.m. prop. ṭoṭā, q.v.
s.m. A parrot.
wety: Possibly from Classical Persian توته (tōta, “parrot”), طوطی (tūtī). Compare Punjabi ਤੋਤਾ (totā) / طوطا (t̤ot̤ā), Sindhi طوطو (t̤ot̤o) / तोतो (toto).

ety: S. also toṭaka, prob. a Prākr̤it form of troṭaka
def: s.m. A particular metre (each line of the stanza containing twelve syllables)
subs: (S. toṭaka+sa-pādika), s.m. A verse of four lines, each containing twenty-five syllables, with a pause at the fourth foot.

def: s.m. A kind of metre consisting of four lines of twelve syllables each.
adj. Lisping, &c. = totlā, q.v.
s.f. = ṭoṅṭī, q.v.
s.f. A parrot; parroquet.
s.m. A charm, spell, an amulet, a philter, a superstitious remedy (cf. ṭāṭak; syn. afsūn).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Articulating imperfectly (as a child), lisping; stuttering, stammering; — lisper; stutterer, stammerer.
v.n. To speak indistinctly, &c. = tutlānā, q.v.
v.t. To weave tape or ribbon.
s.f. Prattling; prattler; (hence) the tongue (cf. totā and totlā).
v.n. = totlānā; tutlānā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of turtle-dove; — the refuse of bhang (in this sense prob. related to S. truṭi; cf. ṭoṭā).
s.m. A kind of turtle-dove; — the refuse of bhang (in this sense prob. related to S. truṭi; cf. ṭoṭā).
s.f. (?), Feast, entertainment.
s.m. Water
subs: s.m. lit. 'Purifying water'; the tree Strychnos potatorum or its nut, the clearing-nut (this nut being rubbed upon the inner surface of a water-jar occasions the precipitation of the impurities of the water poured into it)
s.m. lit. 'Water-giving'; a cloud; the fragrant grass or reed Cyperus rotundus
s.m. 'Bearing or containing water'; a cloud; the pot-herb Marsilia dentata
s.m. 'Receptacle of waters'; the ocean, sea; a reservoir
s.m. 'Fond of water'; a sort of cane growing in or near water, Calamus fasciculatus
s.m. A kind of penance, taking nothing but water for a fixed period
adj. Formed or consisting of water; abounding in water
s.f. A kind of gourd, Momordia charantia.

def: s.f. The thin bark of a species of birch (used to wrap round bows, saddles, &c., and, in Kashmīr, for writing upon in lieu of paper).
s.f. A kind of shawl-stuff manufactured at Toz or Tūz, a town in Khuzistān, in Persia.
intj. Save! deliver! mercy!
subs: v.n. To utter repeated cries for deliverance or mercy
s.m. Crying out for deliverance or mercy.

def: adj. Triple, threefold, consisting of three; divided into three parts; (in comp.) three ( = tri); — s.m. Three collectively, a triad, triplet
subs: traikatva, s.m. The union of three in one, trinity in unity
s.m. The rule of three (in Arith.)
s.f. Three lines, &c. ( = tri-balī); three rows
s.m. The ruler of trilok or the three worlds, an epithet of Indra
s.m. The three sciences; the three Vedas; study or knowledge of the three Vedas; one versed in the three Vedas; one who is acquainted with three sciences.

def: s.m. See s.v. tray or trai for compounds of which trai is the first member.
s.m. Protecting, preserving; protection, safety, preservation, deliverance, salvation; preservative, defence, shelter, help; protection for the body, guard, armour
subs: (˚ṇa+ar˚), s.m. One who is very desirous of protection, &c.
trāṇ-kartā, s.m. Preserver, protector, deliverer, saviour ( = trāṇī)
(-kā), To protect; to free, deliver, save.

def: adj. & s.m. = trāṇī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Protecting, preserving, saving, delivering; pertaining to deliverance, &c.; — protector, preserver, deliverer, saviour.
s.f. Shame; — an unchaste woman (a shame to her family)
s.m. Alarm, fear, dread, terror (syn. bhay)
subs: adj. & s.m. Occasioning alarm or dread, inspiring fear; — terrifier, &c.

def: adj. Afraid, timid, fearful, alarmed.
adj. = trāsā, and trāsī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Terrifying, alarming, frightening; — the act of frightening or alarming; a means of frightening, cause of alarm; fright, alarm.
adj. & s.m. Fearful, timid, afraid ( = trāsā); — a timid person, &c.
